skill.name: tdd-pytest
skill.description: Guides Python test-driven development with pytest, including test design, configuration, coverage runs, and local testing reports.
skill.summary: Use pytest and TDD practices to write, run, audit, and report Python tests.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown backticks as command execution, plus weak cryptography and reconnaissance patterns. Review found these detections are false positives, but the skill intentionally guides pytest command execution and local report/config file edits, so publication should include a runtime warning.

medium_findings.title: Pytest Command Execution Can Run Project Code
medium_findings.description: The skill documents uv run pytest commands. This is legitimate testing behavior, but pytest can execute repository code and plugins in the user workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and match the skill purpose. The risk depends on the repository under test, not on malicious behavior in the skill.

low_findings.title: Local Report And Configuration Writes
low_findings.description: The skill directs creation of TESTING_REPORT.local.md and setup of pyproject.toml. These are expected local writes, but users should review changes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write targets are named directly in the Markdown. They are local project files and do not indicate exfiltration or persistence.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Findings From Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: Most external command detections are inline backticks or fenced Markdown examples for filenames, pytest syntax, fixtures, and TOML configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is inside Markdown documentation, not executable source. No script file, hook, or command wrapper is present.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Crypto And Reconnaissance Detections
low_findings.description: The weak crypto hits point to the skill description and fixture naming. Reconnaissance hits point to TDD text and a pytest exception example.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines do not contain cryptographic algorithms, host discovery, or network scanning behavior. They are ordinary testing documentation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented Test Runner Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes pytest command examples that an agent may run. This is expected, but it can execute code from the active project.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in a bash example block and serve the skill purpose. They are not hidden or obfuscated.

content.value_statement: Python teams need reliable tests that document behavior before implementation. This skill guides pytest TDD workflows, coverage runs, quality audits, and local reports.
actual_capabilities: Guides red-green-refactor workflows for Python code.
Suggests pytest test structures, fixtures, parametrization, async tests, and exception tests.
Provides uv-based pytest commands for targeted runs, verbose output, traceback control, and coverage.
Shows pytest and coverage configuration patterns for pyproject.toml.
Defines contents for TESTING_REPORT.local.md with results, coverage, findings, and recommendations.
Offers conversation guidance for clarifying test scope and edge cases.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not include executable automation scripts.
It assumes pytest, uv, and Python project conventions are available.
Running tests can execute code from the active repository.
It does not replace human review of generated tests or coverage gaps.

use_cases.title: Add Tests Before a Feature
use_cases.description: Define expected behavior, write a failing pytest case, implement the minimum code, and refactor with tests green.

use_cases.title: Audit Existing Test Quality
use_cases.description: Review pytest files for naming, fixture design, parametrization, coverage gaps, and clear recommendations with file references.

use_cases.title: Standardize Project Testing
use_cases.description: Set pytest options in pyproject.toml and produce a local testing report for repeatable project checks.

prompt_templates.title: Write a First Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the tdd-pytest skill to write a failing pytest test for this behavior: [describe behavior]. Show the expected failure first.

prompt_templates.title: Cover Edge Cases
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the tdd-pytest skill to add parametrized pytest cases for [function or feature]. Include normal, boundary, and invalid inputs.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Test Suite
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the tdd-pytest skill to review the tests under [path]. Identify weak assertions, fixture issues, missing cases, and coverage concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Build a TDD Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the tdd-pytest skill to run the relevant pytest commands, assess coverage, and update TESTING_REPORT.local.md with findings and next steps.

output_examples.input: Create tests for a user email validator.
output_examples.output: A focused failing test plan, pytest cases for valid and invalid emails, and a short red-green-refactor implementation path.

output_examples.input: Review my payments tests for quality.
output: Specific recommendations with file and line references.
Coverage and fixture improvements to prioritize.

output_examples.input: Set up pytest for this project.
output_examples.output: A pyproject.toml pytest configuration proposal, coverage settings, and a local testing report outline.

best_practices: Start with the smallest failing test that proves one behavior.
Run targeted pytest commands before full suite and coverage runs.
Review generated tests for clear assertions, useful fixtures, and maintainable naming.
anti_patterns: Do not write broad tests that assert many unrelated behaviors at once.
Do not skip the failing test step when practicing TDD.
Do not run tests from untrusted repositories without reviewing project setup first.

faq.question: Does this skill include executable code?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown guide for pytest workflows and does not ship scripts or binaries.

faq.question: Can it run pytest commands?
faq.answer: It can guide an AI assistant to run pytest commands when the user allows command execution.

faq.question: Does it support uv?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples use uv run pytest for test execution and coverage commands.

faq.question: Can it create pytest configuration?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes pyproject.toml settings for pytest, asyncio, markers, coverage, and warnings.

faq.question: Will it guarantee complete coverage?
faq.answer: No. It helps identify gaps, but developers must confirm requirements and risk areas.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted codebases?
faq.answer: Use caution. Running pytest in an untrusted repository can execute code from that repository.
